summary: monogrok is an obfuscated bulk-email abuse and credential-harvesting toolkit
details: Package ships >200 heavily obfuscated JavaScript modules (obfuscator.io RC4 string-array with rotating parseInt shuffle-guard) implementing a commercial spam/phishing/credential-abuse toolkit. Modules cover bulk SMTP abuse and AWS SES / SendGrid account takeover (func/smx/aws.js, awschk.js, sendgrid.js, sgapi.js, sgchk.js, sgxt.js, passgo.js, vaws.js), EWS/IMAP/POP3 mailbox harvesting (func/box/ewsv.js, imap.js, pop3.js, msap.js, ewsraw.js, owafba.js), Chrome/Discord cookie and credential extraction (func/box/chrm.js, ckie.js, ckpr.js, disc.js), phishing HTML/RTF/XLS document generation (func/gtp/cHtmlRtf.js, cHtmlRtfWImage.js, cHtmlXls.js, encryptHtml.js), LinkedIn/Zoom/Crunchbase/Alibaba scraping (func/emx/lnkdn.js, zoomi.js, alibx.js, crnch.js), Shodan-driven mail-server discovery (func/sho/*), and proxy/IP rotation infrastructure (func/ipr/*, func/rdt/proxy.js). Homepage is mntmc.rip (a license-gated crimeware storefront). Every functional file including the main entry oibWljdTg.js is obfuscated with hex-mangled identifiers and packed string arrays, consistent with hostile distribution to evade static analysis.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
